articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Acrobatic Borre completes Colombia's comeback win over Japan
article.description: Rafael Santos Borre scored with a spectacular overhead kick as Colombia came from a goal down to hand Japan a 2-1 defeat in Osaka on Tuesday.
article.linkText: Borre completes Colombia's comeback over Japan

images.name: Colombia celeb vs. Japan_20230328 [1296x729]
images.alt: afael Santos Borre (C) of Colombia celebrates after scoring the team's second goal with teammates Jhon Arias (L) and Daniel Munoz (R) during the international friendly between Japan and Colombia at Yodoko Sakura Stadium on March 28, 2023 in Osaka, Japan

peers.alt: afael Santos Borre (C) of Colombia celebrates after scoring the team's second goal with teammates Jhon Arias (L) and Daniel Munoz (R) during the international friendly between Japan and Colombia at Yodoko Sakura Stadium on March 28, 2023 in Osaka, Japan

article.story:
Rafael Santos Borre scored with a spectacular overhead kick as Colombia came from a goal down to hand Japan a 2-1 defeat in Osaka on Tuesday.

Kaoru Mitoma had given the Japanese the lead in the third minute as the Brighton & Hove Albion winger rose above the defence to beat Camilo Vargas with a firm downward header.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Jhon Duran equalised with 12 minutes left in the first half when he stroked home following Deiver Machado's cutback in a rare attack for the visitors in the opening 45 minutes.

The Colombians took charge after the interval and Borre struck in the 61st minute, netting from 12 yards out after Daniel Schmidt had blocked Jhon Arias' attempt at goal and the ball had looped up invitingly into the air.

Vargas then denied Japan an equaliser five minutes later with a sprawling save to his left to keep out a goal-bound header from substitute Ayase Ueda.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Japan 1, Colombia 0. Kaoru Mitoma (Japan) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Hidemasa Morita with a cross.
Deiver Machado (Colombia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Yukinari Sugawara (Japan) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Japan 1, Colombia 1. Jhon Durán (Colombia) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Deiver Machado with a cross.
First Half ends, Japan 1, Colombia 1.
Second Half begins Japan 1, Colombia 1.
Substitution, Japan. Wataru Endo replaces Daichi Kamada.
Substitution, Japan. Ayase Ueda replaces Shuto Machino.
Substitution, Japan. Ritsu Doan replaces Kaoru Mitoma.
Substitution, Japan. Ayumu Seko replaces Kashif Bangnagande.
Substitution, Japan. Takefusa Kubo replaces Takuma Nishimura.
Goal!  Japan 1, Colombia 2. Rafael Borré (Colombia) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal.
Substitution, Colombia. Diego Valoyes replaces Jhon Arias.
Substitution, Colombia. Radamel Falcao replaces Rafael Borré.
Substitution, Japan. Takuma Asano replaces Hidemasa Morita.
Substitution, Colombia. Carlos Cuesta replaces Jhon Durán.
Substitution, Colombia. Dylan Borrero replaces Jorge Carrascal.
Diego Valoyes (Colombia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, Japan 1, Colombia 2.
